title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover how to save money and beat crowds during NZ school holidays with our expert travel tips
intro: NZ school holidays can be a challenging time to travel, with crowds and high prices at popular destinations like Rotorua and Queenstown. However, with some planning and insider knowledge, you can still have a fantastic family holiday without breaking the bank. By understanding peak vs off-peak pricing, you can save up to 30% on accommodation and activities.

sections.heading: Plan Ahead and Book Early
sections.body: Booking your accommodation and activities well in advance is crucial to saving money and avoiding crowds. For example, booking a campsite at the popular Whangamata Beach in the Coromandel Peninsula can cost $45 per night during peak season, but prices drop to $25 per night during the off-season. It's also essential to research and book your activities in advance, as popular attractions like the Skyline Gondola in Queenstown can sell out quickly, with prices starting from $44 per adult and $26 per child.

sections.heading: Alternative Destinations
list: Consider visiting lesser-known destinations like the Bay of Islands, which offers a range of activities including dolphin watching and snorkeling, with prices starting from $80 per person.
The Hawke's Bay region is another great option, with its beautiful beaches, wineries, and outdoor activities, and accommodation prices starting from $100 per night.
The West Coast is also a great destination for outdoor enthusiasts, with its stunning landscapes, hiking trails, and scenic drives, and accommodation prices starting from $80 per night.

sections.heading: Drive Times and Distances
sections.body: When planning your road trip, it's essential to consider drive times and distances. For example, the drive from Auckland to Rotorua takes around 3 hours, while the drive from Queenstown to Wanaka takes around 1 hour. Make sure to check the road conditions and weather forecast before embarking on your journey, and consider downloading a traffic app like Waze to help navigate the roads.

sections.heading: Budgeting and Cost-Saving Tips
list: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to help plan and track your expenses.
Consider cooking your own meals instead of eating out, with grocery prices starting from $50 per week for a family of four.
Look for free or low-cost activities, such as visiting local parks and beaches, or taking a scenic drive.

Discover the best destinations for your family holiday

sections.heading: Plan Your Dream Holiday
sections.body: With these tips and insider knowledge, you can plan a fantastic family holiday that suits your budget and preferences. Remember to always research and book in advance, and consider alternative destinations and activities to avoid the crowds. Happy travels!
